Module 4:
Your Pre-Prelaunch, Part 2
!
Executive Summary
!
Four main areas of the Pre-Prelaunch:
• 	Identifying the offer, the story, and the objections
• 	Building early buzz
• 	Warming your list
• 	Pre-prelaunch assets and planning
!
Part 1: Identifying the offer, the story, and the objections
• 	Finding out what they want
• 	The “Shot Across the Bow”
• 	“Testimonial Cruncher” - this is where you lead them through a
testimonial funnel and ask for tiny commitments along the way
• 	You can do this through SurveyMonkey or SurveyGizmo
• 	Example of the Testimonial Cruncher in action:
• 	Sent an email to both prospects and owners
• 	“Quick announcement and a favor”
• 	Announces new product that’s almost ready
• 	Sends them to a short survey and promises more information
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 2 --

• 	Survey explains details on the upcoming product and asks two
questions that help make the product better
• 	Second page of survey asks for contact information
• 	Third page asks them for a testimonial - offers some examples
• 	This is the “Testimonial Cruncher” - they clicked through to the
survey, filled it out, gave contact information, and then ask for the
testimonial
• 	They’re already moving down the path and they’re more inclined
to offer the testimonial
This is a great technique if you’re just starting out - it doesn't have to be
strictly about what you’re selling either.
Modeling other testimonials gives them guidelines on what makes a strong
one. It’s a very powerful way to capture testimonials, which will greatly help
your launch.
!
